prompt: Find where ImmutableList is implemented in this repository. Show the main source file, the builder implementation, and the most relevant test files. Explain the relationship between them.
Find the implementation of CacheBuilder and trace how it leads to a concrete cache implementation at runtime. List the most important classes involved.
Locate the code that implements Range and explain which classes handle contiguous ranges, cuts, and comparisons.
Trace how LoadingCache.get(K) works internally in Guava. Start from the public API and walk through the main internal call path until the value is loaded.
Find where Futures.transform is implemented and identify the classes responsible for scheduling and executing the transformation callback.
Show me the call chain from Multimaps.index to the data structures it returns. Explain which collection types are created underneath.
I want to understand Splitter behavior. Find the most relevant production files and the best tests that document tricky edge cases. Summarize the edge cases covered by the tests.
Find tests for ImmutableSet.Builder duplicate handling and explain what behavior is guaranteed.
Find the best tests covering RateLimiter warmup behavior and explain what scenarios they validate.
Explain how Guava’s graph APIs are organized. Identify the main public interfaces, key implementations, and any immutable or mutable variants. Point me to the most central files.
Give me a high-level map of Guava’s caching subsystem. Group the important classes by role: public API, builders/config, core cache logic, eviction/maintenance, and tests.
Explain the package structure around com.google.common.collect. Which files look foundational, and which are convenience utilities layered on top?
